title: Update Dockerfile to use golang:1.12 image
body: Hey, I simply updated the image and switched to the official seccomp packages, which should now be fulfilling since the golang images are based on stretch.

comments.body:
Hi @saschagrunert. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

/approve

Note that the CAH and FAH28 tests are currently flaky. Changes LGTM.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *saschagrunert*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Papr is failing, but papr is failing everywhere. LGTM once the remove papr PR lands (you'll need to rebase once it does to fix CI)
@saschagrunert Can you rebase on top of master to fix the failing Papr tests?
Is there a reason we're doing a specific libseccomp commit? We could just as easily pin master, given how slowly the library updates
I rebased on top of the latest master branch.

> I feel this creates diversion between behaviour of building podman on `host` vs `within container`.
> 
> On host, we checkout [`seccomp` with specific git version](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/278c961f9814b6dde1cb52b017e4afe52f4ad98b/Makefile#L325). The same shall be followed in `Dockerfile` too.

This should not be a problem. Any distribution package of Podman will be built using the distribution's seccomp package, not the downloaded version in the Dockerfile. The `install.libseccomp.sudo` target is an optional target and not mandatory for a build. I assume that target in the Makefile has also been added for compilation on older versions of Ubuntu.

>This should not be a problem. Any distribution package of Podman will be built using the distribution's seccomp package, not the downloaded version in the Dockerfile. The install.libseccomp.sudo target is an optional target and not mandatory for a build

Agree.  If there is no specific dependencies on `LIBSECCOMP_COMMIT` it can be removed or atleast a comment (stating requiremnt/optional) in Makefile shall be helpful
